overview: A stateless web app where users upload a bike photo, Claude Vision analyzes it and returns structured part/condition/priority data, and the backend scrapes Performance Bicycle for real pricing on identified parts. No persistence, no auth, local deployment only.
goals: Accept a bike photo upload and store it as a temp file
Send the image to Claude Vision and receive structured JSON with parts, condition, priority, and bike metadata
Scrape Performance Bicycle for real pricing and availability on each identified part
Render a clean desktop-first results page showing analysis and pricing
nonGoals: No image storage beyond temp files
No user accounts or saved analyses (Phase 3)
successMetrics: Photo uploads successfully and reaches the backend
Claude Vision returns valid structured JSON matching the defined schema
At least one Performance Bicycle pricing result renders per identified part
Full analysis and pricing results display cleanly on a 1280px desktop viewport
openQuestions: Should scraping failures for individual parts be silent (show 'unavailable') or surface an error?
Is there a maximum number of parts to scrape pricing for per analysis (rate/time concern)?

stack.vision: Claude Vision API (claude-sonnet-4-5 or latest model with vision)

routes.name: Home / Upload
routes.purpose: Photo upload form; entry point for analysis

routes.name: Results
routes.purpose: Displays bike metadata, parts analysis, and Performance Bicycle pricing

uiNotes: Desktop-first layout, minimum 1280px comfortable; responsive at ≥768px
Upload page: centered card with drag-and-drop or file picker, upload button
Results page: two-column layout — left: uploaded photo + bike metadata; right: scrollable parts table with condition badge, priority badge, and pricing
Condition badges: excellent=green, good=blue, fair=yellow, poor=red
Loading state shown while analysis and scraping are in progress
Error state shown if Claude or scraping fails

importFormat.description: Not applicable — input is a photo file upload (multipart/form-data), not structured data

claudeVisionSchema.description: Structured JSON Claude Vision must return. Backend validates this shape before scraping.

bike.notes: Clean frame with minor handlebar tape wear

parts.name: Rear Derailleur
parts.condition_notes: Visible rust on jockey wheels, cable slightly frayed

example.summary: Road bike in generally good condition. Drivetrain shows wear and should be serviced soon.

priorityScale.1: Immediate — safety concern or broken, replace now
priorityScale.2: Soon — significant wear, replace within a month
priorityScale.3: Monitor — showing wear, keep an eye on it
priorityScale.4: OK — good condition, no action needed
priorityScale.5: New / Excellent — recently replaced or like new

rules: Claude Vision prompt must instruct the model to return only valid JSON matching the defined schema — no prose
Backend must validate Claude's response matches the schema before proceeding to scrape
If a part's condition is 'unknown', it should still be included in results but scraping is skipped for that part
Scraping is performed per part using the part's search_query field
Temp image files must be deleted after the analysis completes (success or failure)
Performance Bicycle scraper returns max 3 results per part to keep response time acceptable
If scraping for a part fails, return an empty results array for that part — do not fail the whole request

stories.title: [Setup] Project scaffold — monorepo structure, scripts, and env config
stories.description: As a developer, I want the project folder structure, scripts, and environment configuration in place so all three work trees can begin in parallel.
acceptanceCriteria: Root directory contains /client (React/Vite) and /server (Express) folders
Root package.json includes scripts: `dev` (starts both client and server), `lint`, `test`
server/package.json includes dependencies: express, multer, cors, dotenv, axios, cheerio — install with `npm install express multer cors dotenv axios cheerio`
client/package.json scaffolded via Vite: `npm create vite@latest client -- --template react`; install with `npm install`
server/.env.example contains ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key_here and CLIENT_URL=http://localhost:5173
server/.env is gitignored
README.md documents: how to set env vars, how to run `npm run dev` from root
Example: cloning repo, copying .env.example to .env, running npm run dev — both client and server start without errors
Negative case: missing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env causes server startup to log a clear warning (not a crash)

stories.title: [Tree 1 — Backend] Express server with health check and CORS
stories.description: As a developer, I want a running Express server with CORS configured and a health check endpoint so the frontend can communicate with it.
acceptanceCriteria: Express server starts on PORT from .env (default 3001)
CORS configured to allow requests from CLIENT_URL env var
GET /api/health returns { status: 'ok', timestamp: ISO-string }
Server uses dotenv to load environment variables on startup
npm test includes a test that GET /api/health returns 200 with status 'ok'
Example: curl http://localhost:3001/api/health → 200 { status: 'ok' }
Negative case: request from disallowed origin receives CORS error

stories.title: [Tree 1 — Backend] Photo upload endpoint with multer temp storage
stories.description: As a user, I want to POST a bike photo to the backend so it can be processed by Claude Vision.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/analyze accepts multipart/form-data with a field named `image`
multer stores the uploaded file in OS temp directory (os.tmpdir())
Accepted file types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp — reject others with 400
Max file size: 10MB — reject larger files with 413 and clear error message
File is deleted from temp storage after response is sent (success or error)
npm install multer already included in US-001
Example: POST with a valid JPEG → file saved to temp, path available in req.file
Negative case: POST with a .pdf file → 400 'Unsupported file type'
Negative case: POST with a 15MB image → 413 'File too large'

stories.title: [Tree 1 — Backend] Claude Vision API integration with structured JSON output
stories.description: As the backend, I want to send the uploaded image to Claude Vision and receive a validated structured JSON analysis of the bike's parts, condition, and metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Install Anthropic SDK: `npm install @anthropic-ai/sdk` in /server
Image is read from temp file and base64-encoded before sending to Claude
Claude is called with model `claude-sonnet-4-5` (or latest vision-capable model)
System prompt instructs Claude to return ONLY valid JSON — no markdown, no prose, no code fences

Prompt specifies the full schema: bike object (brand, model, type, year, color, frame_material, notes) and parts array (id, name, component_group, brand, model, condition, condition_notes, priority 1-5, priority_label, search_query, visible_in_image)
Prompt includes the priority scale (1=Immediate, 2=Soon, 3=Monitor, 4=OK, 5=New) and condition values (excellent/good/fair/poor/unknown)
Prompt includes component group taxonomy: Drivetrain, Brakes, Wheels, Handlebars/Cockpit, Saddle/Seatpost, Frame/Fork, Accessories
Backend validates response is parseable JSON and contains required top-level keys: bike, parts, overall_condition, summary
Invalid or unparseable Claude response returns 502 with error message
Example: valid bike photo → Claude returns JSON with bike metadata and 5-10 parts
Negative case: Claude returns non-JSON text → backend returns 502 'Analysis failed: invalid response format'

stories.title: [Tree 2 — Scraping] Performance Bicycle part search scraper
stories.description: As the backend, I want to search Performance Bicycle by part name and scrape real pricing and availability so users see current market prices.
acceptanceCriteria: cheerio is installed in /server (included in US-001 setup)
scrapePartPricing(searchQuery) function accepts a search query string and returns an array of up to 3 results
Each result contains: { title: string, price: string, availability: string, url: string }
HTTP request uses a realistic User-Agent header to avoid simple bot blocks
If no results are found, return an empty array (not an error)
If the HTTP request fails (network error, non-200 status), return an empty array and log the error
Function is exported from server/src/scrapers/performanceBicycle.js
npm test includes a test that scrapePartPricing returns an array (may be empty if site unreachable — test shape, not content)
Example: scrapePartPricing('Shimano Claris rear derailleur 8-speed') → [{title, price, availability, url}, ...]
Negative case: scrapePartPricing('xyzzy nonexistent part 9999') → []

stories.title: [Tree 2 — Scraping] Pricing API endpoint — batch pricing for all parts
stories.description: As the backend, I want a POST /api/pricing endpoint that accepts an array of parts and returns scraped pricing for each one.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/pricing accepts JSON body: { parts: [{ id, name, search_query }] }
Scrapes Performance Bicycle for each part using its search_query field
Returns JSON: { pricing: [{ part_id, part_name, search_query, results: [{title, price, availability, url}] }] }
Parts are scraped sequentially (not concurrently) to avoid hammering the target site
Endpoint returns 200 even if all parts have empty pricing results
Returns 400 if parts array is missing or empty
npm test includes a test verifying POST /api/pricing with valid input returns 200 with correct shape
Example: POST with 3 parts → response contains 3 pricing entries (results may be empty arrays)
Negative case: POST with missing parts field → 400 'parts array is required'

stories.title: [Tree 3 — Frontend] React + Vite frontend scaffold with routing
stories.description: As a developer, I want a working React frontend with client-side routing so I can build Upload and Results pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Install react-router-dom: `npm install react-router-dom` in /client
App.jsx defines routes: / → UploadPage, /results → ResultsPage
Both UploadPage and ResultsPage are stub components that render a heading (to be fleshed out in later stories)
Vite dev server runs on port 5173 without errors
Global CSS reset applied (box-sizing border-box, margin 0, base font)
npm run lint passes with no errors
Example: navigating to / renders 'Upload Page', navigating to /results renders 'Results Page'
Negative case: unknown route (e.g. /foo) renders a 404 'Page not found' message

stories.title: [Tree 3 — Frontend] Photo upload form component
stories.description: As a user, I want to select or drag-and-drop a bike photo on the upload page so I can trigger an analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: UploadPage renders a centered card with a drag-and-drop zone and a file picker fallback
Accepted file types restricted to image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp in the input element
After selecting a file, a preview thumbnail of the image is shown
An 'Analyze Bike' button is enabled only when a file is selected
Submitting the form calls POST /api/analyze (via fetch or axios) with multipart/form-data
While the request is in flight, button shows 'Analyzing…' and is disabled
On success, navigate to /results passing the response data via React Router state
On error, display an inline error message: 'Analysis failed. Please try again.'
Install axios in /client: `npm install axios`
Example: drag a JPEG onto the zone → thumbnail appears → click Analyze → loading state shown
Negative case: attempt to upload a .gif → browser file picker rejects it due to accept attribute

stories.title: [Tree 3 — Frontend] Analysis results display component
stories.description: As a user, I want to see my bike's metadata and a detailed parts analysis table so I know what needs attention.
acceptanceCriteria: ResultsPage reads analysis data from React Router location.state
Bike metadata section shows: brand, model, type, year, color, frame_material, overall_condition, summary
Parts section renders a table with columns: Part Name, Component Group, Brand/Model, Condition (badge), Priority (badge), Condition Notes
Condition badges: excellent=green, good=blue, fair=yellow, poor=red
Priority badges: 1=red 'Immediate', 2=orange 'Soon', 3=yellow 'Monitor', 4=blue 'OK', 5=green 'New'
Parts table is sortable by Priority (default sort: lowest number first = most urgent)
If location.state is null or missing, redirect to / with a message 'No analysis data — please upload a photo'
Example: results page loaded with 8 parts → table shows all 8 sorted by priority
Negative case: results page navigated to directly with no state → redirects to upload page

stories.title: [Tree 3 — Frontend] Pricing results display — integrated into results page
stories.description: As a user, I want to see Performance Bicycle pricing for each identified part so I know what replacements will cost.
acceptanceCriteria: After analysis data loads, ResultsPage automatically calls POST /api/pricing with the parts array
While pricing is loading, each part row shows a 'Loading prices…' skeleton or spinner
Once pricing loads, each part row expands to show up to 3 Performance Bicycle results: product title, price, availability, and a link to the product page
If a part has no pricing results, show 'No results found on Performance Bicycle'
Pricing load errors are shown inline per part: 'Pricing unavailable'
Layout remains clean and scannable at 1280px width
Example: 6-part analysis → pricing call fires → each part row shows 1-3 product options with prices
Negative case: pricing API returns empty array for a part → 'No results found on Performance Bicycle' shown for that row

stories.title: [Integration] End-to-end flow — upload photo, get analysis, show pricing
stories.description: As a user, I want the full workflow to work end-to-end: upload a photo, see Claude's analysis, and see real pricing from Performance Bicycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Upload page successfully POSTs to POST /api/analyze and receives valid analysis JSON
POST /api/analyze calls Claude Vision and returns the structured bike/parts JSON
ResultsPage calls POST /api/pricing after receiving analysis data
Full flow completes without console errors in browser or server
Temp image file is cleaned up server-side after analysis
Tested manually: upload a real bike photo → full results render with bike metadata, parts table, and pricing
Example: upload Trek road bike photo → see 5-10 identified parts with conditions and Performance Bicycle prices
Negative case: upload a non-bike photo (e.g. a cat) → Claude still returns valid JSON (may have few/no parts), app renders gracefully without crashing

stories.title: [Integration] Desktop-first layout polish and basic styling
stories.description: As a user, I want the app to look clean and professional on desktop so the results are easy to read and act on.
acceptanceCriteria: App has a consistent color palette and typography (system font stack acceptable)
Upload page: centered card, max-width 600px, subtle shadow, clear call to action
Results page: two-column layout at ≥1024px (photo + bike info left, parts table right), single column below 768px
Parts table has alternating row colors for readability
Condition and priority badges are visually distinct and legible
Loading states (analysis + pricing) are clearly communicated with a spinner or skeleton
No horizontal scroll at 1280px
npm run lint passes with no errors after styling changes
Example: load results page at 1280px → clean two-column layout with no overflow
Negative case: view at 375px (mobile) → layout stacks to single column, no elements overflow viewport
